questions.question: What is the difference between a pure and impure pipe?
options: Pure pipes are faster; impure pipes are more accurate
A pure pipe only re-runs when the input reference changes; an impure pipe re-runs on every change detection cycle
Pure pipes return strings; impure pipes can return any type
Pure pipes work with primitives; impure pipes work with objects and arrays

questions.question: You're building a standalone component that needs the date pipe. What must you add to the imports array?
options: DateModule
BrowserModule
CommonModule (which includes all built-in pipes) or DatePipe directly
AngularPipes

questions.question: What does the async pipe do that makes it better than manual subscribe in components?
options: It makes HTTP requests faster by using a web worker
It automatically subscribes when the component is created and unsubscribes when destroyed — preventing memory leaks
It batches multiple Observable emissions into one template update
It converts the Observable to a Promise for simpler Angular template syntax

questions.question: How do you chain two pipes — first truncate to 20 chars, then uppercase?
options: {{ value | truncate(20) | uppercase }}
{{ value | truncate:20 | uppercase }}
{{ value.truncate(20).toUpperCase() }}
{{ value | [truncate:20, uppercase] }}

questions.question: A TimeAgoPipe shows '5 minutes ago' but the display never updates. What is the likely fix?
options: Inject ChangeDetectorRef and call markForCheck() every second
Change the pipe to pure: false — Angular's change detection will re-run impure pipes automatically
Use OnPush change detection strategy on the component
Subscribe to a timer Observable and manually call transform() in the component
